steps.title: Understanding when a perfect square equals a constant
steps.content:
All equations have the form $(x+15)^2 = k$. For a perfect square equal to a constant:
  • If $k = 0$: the square equals zero at exactly one point, giving one solution
  • If $k > 0$: taking square roots gives $x + 15 = \pm\sqrt{k}$, yielding two distinct solutions
  • If $k < 0$: no real solution exists, since a real number squared cannot be negative

steps.title: Evaluating the constant in each choice
steps.content:
Extract the constant $k$ from the right side of each equation:
  • A) $k = 0$ ✓ exactly one solution
  • B) $k = -45$ → no real solutions
  • C) $k = 45$ → two solutions
  • D) $k = 135$ → two solutions
Only choice A yields exactly one distinct real solution. That matches choice A — A is correct.
